subject: a093b1df791839bd5
findings: All three review-round-1 findings closed. C4R-1 (Major) closed with TestShopDefaultBudgetThreadsThroughToTheWire, a server-boundary test asserting the wire dispatches lane (not the fold package) carries state=overdue, budget_source=default, budget_seconds=1800 when Config.DefaultsPath is set - red-first proven by guarding poll.go's WithDefaultBudgetSeconds call locally, observing the stated RED (wire state=dispatched instead of overdue), and restoring byte-identical. C4R-2 (Minor) closed - the three dead sse_test.go citations now point at TestSnapshotAndStreamShareOneMarshalPath and TestSSEEventNameMatchesSchemaConstant; a full sweep of every test-file and Test-identifier citation across board/server, board/store, board/assemble, and docs/contracts found no other dead reference. C4R-3 (Minor) closed with TestChangeDetectionFiresOnAgeBucketBoundaryCrossing, proven by fault-injecting ageBucketMs out of the comparison and observing the server incorrectly keep serving the first poll's stale snapshot, then reverting byte-identical. A genuine new finding surfaced while building the C4R-3 test and is disclosed, not silently fixed: mustMarshalStripped does not strip a dispatched-winner's elapsed_seconds, which unlike ageBucketMs is unquantised, so a live train with an active dispatched car will bump seq on nearly every poll independent of a real state change - recorded in poll.go's doc comment and docs/contracts/gating-matrix.md as a decision for the conductor's triage. All fault-injections were reverted to a byte-identical diff before being confirmed green, verified via git diff.
abstract: Fix cycle round 2 for Car 4 (the yard-board server car) closed one Major and two Minor findings from review round 1 (artifacts/reviews/2026-07-23-board-car4-review-round1-REJECT.md) in commit 5dd1fc8 on car/board-car4, HEAD verified at 099ac49 before starting. The Major closed a real regression-coverage gap - the shop-default-budget threading the reviewer proved works live had zero committed test at the server boundary - with a new test that would catch a future refactor deleting the threading. Both Minors closed cleanly, and building the C4R-3 test surfaced a genuine unstripped-elapsed_seconds churn issue that was disclosed rather than silently patched, since fixing it was outside this cycle's stated scope. Every fix was proven red-first via local fault injection (guard/strip the mechanism, observe red for the stated reason, revert to a git-diff-empty state, confirm green) before landing. Final state: go test -count=1 ./... all 5 packages green (server now 26 tests), go vet clean, gofmt -l empty, Pester 235/235 unchanged, working tree clean.
